Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

brhad/laghu bhagatamrta

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2010-08-18 · 2 answers
Some of the information in brhad/laghu bhagatamrta are "far out" and very hard to even imagine.

Multiple Brahmas - Number of heads in Lord Brahma depends on size of the Universe - Multiple Sivas etc.

Are similar details available in vedic literature such as Puranas OR should these be considered "realized knowledge" by Goswamis and we they are very merciful to pass them to us ?
user [560] · 2010-08-21
I think that if it is like that expresed within the concept in Vedanta of the external causes pertaining the whole universal manifestation, it is as exponed inside the "Krsna Book" that is say in Ch.10 on Srimad Bhagavatam. About the liberation of Lord Siva Chapter it is said that it is Lord Visnu who is the One and only Internal cause and therefore expressed the primeval and primordial beyond the externals causes and shall read good from the begining of the chapter do explaining why are in an inverse situation the devotees of Lord Visnu and the devotees of Lord Siva.. intertwined Siva not being opulent and Visnu being the most opulent.., Their devotees are unlinkely, like vaisnavas are more simple living austere and the devotees of Lord Siva are being living very opulently. Maharaja Pariksit'b4s question is very much answered by Srila Sukadeva Gosvami.

Now If it is not wrong nor clear as found in Veda that there is One Brahma who'b4s said to have 5 heads and Lord Siva do cuts His 5th leaving brahma with 4 heads, and I can not recall this pastime right now maybe VEDA. And I also understood that There are manifested the 3 aspects of Visnu on how does they expand from the Original Lord Krsna'b4s spiritual body in Goloka Vrindavana. And that also leaves us to try to conclude that there is only one Siva for each and every universe created who takes under control the whole affair to dissolute and encounter destruction cosmic. Correct me if I am wrong conditioned, Hari Bol.

From that point I beleive it was Sivarama Swami Maharaja once in Mayapur who gave a class on Tamal Krsna Gosvami disappearance day that explains that we can not confuse this two books the Laghu with the Brhat Bhagavatamrtams and He was explaining on how talking about Lord Varaha'b4s incarnation and also reffering that at the same time on how there are different fur colours of the Lords Boar incarnation that appears also at the same time to how and why.

I beleive that you can find the video on Youtube.. about two years ago. Very nice talk..

Hare Krsna
user [38] · 2010-08-19
I checked my Purana editions and found nothing about Brahmas, Sivas, etc. from other universes.
